title: Accounts & authentication with signed bearer tokens
body:
## Goal
Let users register and log in, and give every protected endpoint a reusable guard (imported directly from `src/auth.js` by each route module, not injected via server ctx) that authenticates the caller from a signed bearer token with a configurable expiry.

## Changes
- {"path":"src/auth.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/routes/auth.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"tests/auth.test.js","assumption":"creates"}

## Acceptance
- [ ] `POST /auth/register` with `{username, password}` creates a user and responds with `{id, username}`; the password (or its hash) never appears in any response.
- [ ] Registering a username that already exists is rejected with a 4xx JSON error rather than creating a second account.
- [ ] `POST /auth/login` with valid credentials responds with `{token}`, a bearer token signed with a secret key; logging in with invalid credentials returns 401 and no token.
- [ ] A request to a bearer-guarded endpoint with a missing, malformed, or tampered token returns HTTP 401 with a structured JSON error.
- [ ] A token whose `exp` is in the past is rejected with HTTP 401 — the token-signing helper exposes a configurable TTL/expiry so `tests/auth.test.js` can forge an already-expired token deterministically.
- [ ] `node --test tests/auth.test.js` exits 0.

## Verify
- node --test tests/auth.test.js (contract)
- npm run typecheck (validate)

> 🏷️ Authored with Mandrel v0.0.0 · 2026-07-12

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Own the authentication boundary — password hashing, HMAC token sign/verify, and the requireAuth guard — as one revertable unit (b: isolates the security-critical auth boundary into its own reviewable Story).","mandrel_version":"0.0.0","authored_at":"2026-07-12"} -->
